title: Dacryodes pubescens
taxonomy: Dacryodes pubescens is a species of plant in the family Burseraceae. The plant is native to tropical regions and is commonly found in Central and South America. It is classified as a member of the order Sapindales. The classification of Dacryodes pubescens places it within the family of plants commonly known as torchwood trees. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its botanical features.

The bark of the tree is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In traditional medicine, the leaves are used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
